title: Queues, Streams, Super Streams, Retention, and Replay · Contract Lens: Stream Client Locality
summary: A high-throughput replay workload uses one quorum queue with a ten-million-message backlog, while another service selects streams but expects destructive consumption and ordinary queue client locality. Planned artifact: Add and verify the stream client locality increment in queue-versus-stream workload decision and replay plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Account for stream protocol, replica locality, leader and follower reads, connection routing, confirms, and failure recovery.
Use super streams to partition throughput by key while accounting for partitions, routing, per-partition order, skew, and consumer coordination.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Stream Client Locality · Theory Stream Client Locality
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 5447 in a high-volume telemetry route with one hot customer key. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to choose queue, stream, or super stream from replay, backlog, throughput, and order-key evidence; retain duplicate-safe identity through every ambiguous handoff; then reuse one message identity with a different payload fingerprint. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Stream Client Locality contract for the supplied data.
